Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Be Relieved From Deemed Admissions (Ccp 2033.300(A)); Points And Authorities; Declaration
Off Calendar Feb-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

Ruling - Plaintiff Adam Makubuya's Motion To Be Relieved From Deemed Admissions (Ccp 2033.300(A)).

There being no appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted: Hearing required at which moving party must provide a proof of service of this motion on the defendant's counsel, as none is available on the court's docket. If the motion was not timely served, it will be denied due to improper service. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive protion of the tentative rulig.

Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT/HK)

Heidi C. Quan, Esq. of Murchison & Cumming LLP, T:415.524.4300, for plaintiff/moving party;
Adam Makubuya, T:650.703.6537, plaintiff/opposing party, appearing on his own behalf.

DEFENDANT GEORGE COOK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT In Favor Of George Cook Dba Bryant Transmission Center

RULING: The Court instructed Mr. Makubuya to file his opposition papers in the proper form and to provide copies to the Court and plaintiff's counsel. The Court then continues the hearing on DEFENDANT GEORGE COOK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT In Favor Of George Cook Dba Bryant Transmission Center to February 28, 2018 at 9:30 am in Department 302, with the following briefing schedule: Opposition must be filed by 2/16/18; reply papers must be filed by 2/23/18.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT In Favor Of George Cook Dba Bryant Transmission Center
Off Calendar Feb-05-2018 Continued To Feb-07-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adam Makubuya, T:650.703.6537, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf;
No other appearance.

RULING: Plaintiff presents to the court a copy of his response to the motion for summary judgment. The Court instructs the clerk to file the response and provide a copy to defendant's counsel by email. The Court also vacates the tentative ruling and continues DEFENDANT GEORGE COOK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT In Favor Of George Cook dba Bryant Transmission Center to February 7, 2018 at 9:30 am. The clerk provided notice of the continued hearing date to defendant's counsel by email.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

MOTION For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Heidi C. Quan In Support
Off Calendar Nov-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Heidi Quan, Esq. 415-524-4300 of Murchison & Cumming LLP for Defendant/moving party.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GEORGE COOK MOTION For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff.

The unopposed motion of Defendant George Cook, dba Bryant Transmision Center, for an order that Defendant's unanswered Requests for Admission, Set One, by deemed admitted, and for discovery sanctions, is granted. Plaintiff is deemed to have admitted each Request for Admission (1 through 4) set forth in the Requests. Plaintiff is ordered to pay Defendant discovery sanctions in the amount of $769.50, within thirty days of entry of this order.

Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/JPT/HK)

Off Calendar Apr-27-2017 Sustained

ATTY. KATELYN KNIGHT OF THE MURCHISON AND CUNNING LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-513-0477 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - PLAINTIFF WAS REQUESTING COURT FOR A CONTINUATION OF THE MATTER IN ORDER FOR HIM TO FIND AN ATTORNEY BY JUNE 2, 2017. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: Defendant George Cook dba Bryant Transmission Center's demurrer to all causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Adam Makubuya is sustained with leave to amend no later than June 2, 2017.
